themes.exampleQuote: I always make sure to try their new flavors—because it always surprises me!
I loved the waffle cones which they make fresh throughout the day.
The portion of these two 'standards' is mind boggling—never received a portion so small for $7.
Me and my dog's favorite spot—I always order him a small scoop of vanilla with waffle wedge.
That gooey butter cake ice cream has me in a chokehold.

notable_quote: I loved the waffle cones which they make fresh throughout the day.
sentiment_summary: Reviewers keep coming back for the rotating flavor program and freshly made waffle cones, but a recurring complaint about portion size relative to price—specifically the $7 two-scoop standard—chips away at an otherwise warm reception.
quarter_narrative: Twenty-nine Pittsburgh creators filmed inside Jeni's this quarter and walked out with 147,000 plays; Jeni's itself posted none of it back. The top video—@justapittsburghgirl's gooey butter cake reel with 48,400 views—is doing promotional work the brand isn't doing for itself, and that gap is the defining story of Jeni's Spring 2026. In the Pittsburgh Sweets family, Page's holds the top signal position on the back of social momentum Jeni's currently can't match, despite sitting on a creator network most shops in this city would trade everything for. The rank moves when the business stops being a subject and starts being a participant.
tldr_read: Twenty-nine creators are filming this place; the place has no TikTok of its own—Icons tier, #2 Pittsburgh Ice Cream.
tldr_meaning: The conversion path and creator ecosystem are fully intact; the only axis dragging rank is the absence of the business's own voice on the platform where the city is already talking about it.

diagnosis_pullquote.line: Twenty-Nine Creators Filming, Zero TikToks Back—The City Sees Jeni's Clearer Than Jeni's Sees Itself.

playbook.headline: Claim the TikTok the city built you
playbook.action: Open a business TikTok account and repost the 30+ creator videos already tagged to this location—starting with @justapittsburghgirl's 48,400-play gooey butter cake reel.

playbook.headline: Address the portion complaint directly
playbook.action: Review sentiment sits below the family median of 83; the $7 two-scoop portion complaint appears across multiple reviews and is the single most consistent drag on star distribution.

playbook.headline: Turn the flavor calendar into a visual asset
playbook.action: Instagram is posting 12 times a month but engagement sits at 0.49%; flavor drops and rotating seasonal specials are the obvious recurring visual hook that's currently underleveraged in the feed.
